draft.introduction: Mark’s account of the Last Supper, the earliest narrative of the institution of the Eucharist. The words “this is my body” and “this is my blood of the covenant” became the most debated sentences in Christian theology.

translation.text: And as they were eating, he took bread, and after blessing it broke it and gave it to them, and said, “Take; this is my body.” And he took a cup, and when he had given thanks he gave it to them, and they all drank of it. And he said to them, “This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many.”
translation.original_text: τοῦτό ἐστιν τὸ σῶμά μου ... τοῦτό ἐστιν τὸ αἷμά μου τῆς διαθήκης

translator_notes.note: Sinaiticus reads “blood of the covenant” (αἷμά μου τῆς διαθήκης) without the word “new” found in some later manuscripts. The allusion is to Exodus 24:8 where Moses sprinkles the “blood of the covenant” on the people at Sinai.
